title: Mini Farmhouse Bedside Table
summary: A small farmhouse nightstand with a 2x2-leg frame, one drawer, and a planked 1x6 top — sized to fit tight bedroom corners without giving up a drawer.
description:
This is a farmhouse nightstand scaled down deliberately — the whole footprint is built around a single 1x12 side panel, so the table stays small enough for a tight bedroom corner without sacrificing the one thing a nightstand really needs: a drawer. The side panels get trimmed first, on the bench, before they ever meet the legs — building trim onto a flat panel first is far more accurate than trying to trim a panel that's already boxed into a leg frame.

The drawer bottom is a deliberate cost-saver: rather than buying separate stock for it, the plan reuses another 1x12, keeping the whole materials list to a handful of board types you're already buying for the rest of the table. Set the drawer slides so the drawer sits about 1/8" proud, then adjust until it moves easily — and the plan's own tip is worth taking seriously: a light rub of candle wax on the slide contact points measurably improves how a drawer glides, for the cost of nothing.

The optional 1/4" plywood back isn't just a design nicety; it's what stiffens a frame this size against the wobble a nightstand takes from someone reaching over for their phone every morning. Skip it only if the table will sit flush against a wall its whole life.

materials.name: 1x12, 4 ft
materials.note: Sides, bottom shelf, and the drawer bottom.

materials.name: 2x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, side stretchers, and front trim.

cutList.note: Match to the actual width of your 1x12.

steps.title: Trim the side panels
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim to the side 1x12 pieces using a nailer from the inside, which keeps nail holes hidden. Use 1-1/4" nails and glue. Do this for both sides.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the trimmed side panels with a pocket-hole jig set for 1-1/2" pocket holes on 2x2-to-2x2 joints and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws. For any joints involving the 1x boards, switch to the 3/4" setting with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Do this for both sides.

steps.title: Connect the sides
steps.body: Connect the two side assemblies together with the front and back trim boards.

steps.title: Add the back panel
steps.body: Attach the optional 1/4" plywood back panel with nails and glue — it stiffens the frame considerably against the daily reach-over wobble a nightstand takes.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Build the drawer box. The bottom is just another piece of 1x12, which keeps the materials list simple — attach it on the underside with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue.

steps.title: Install the slides and drawer face
steps.body: Install the drawer slides so the drawer face sits down about 1/8" from the case front, leaving an even gap on all sides. Adjust the slides until the drawer moves easily — a light rub of candle wax on the contact points noticeably improves the glide.

steps.title: Add the front trim
steps.body: Trim out the front of the table with the remaining 2x2 stock.

steps.title: Build and attach the top and bottom shelf
steps.body: Build the top and the bottom shelf the same way, with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, then attach the bottom shelf on top of its stretchers. Sand and finish the whole table.

images.alt: Small farmhouse-style bedside table with a drawer and planked top
